residual_risks.description: When the Vault fallback recovers a plaintext secret, `saveProviderApiKey` re-writes that same secret back to Vault via `storeCredential` (line 576 of `saveProviderApiKey`'s body) before re-encrypting locally. Since the secret was just read from Vault, this is an idempotent but unnecessary network call on every self-heal. Not a type-safety issue, but worth noting as an architectural wart — a narrower re-encrypt-only helper that skips the Vault write would avoid the redundant round-trip.
